name: flatppl
description: Syntax highlighting and DAG visualization for the FlatPPL probabilistic language

scripts.install:vsix: npm run package:vsix && { command -v code >/dev/null 2>&1 && code --install-extension ../../flatppl-vscode-extension-nightly.vsix --force || echo "warning: 'code' not on PATH; packaged vsix at flatppl-vscode-extension-nightly.vsix — install with: code --install-extension <path>"; }

commandPalette.when: editorLangId == flatppl || editorLangId == python || editorLangId == julia
editorLangId == flatppl || editorLangId == python || editorLangId == julia
editorLangId == flatppl || ((editorLangId == python || editorLangId == julia) && flatppl.embeddedActive)

editor/title.when: editorLangId == flatppl || ((editorLangId == python || editorLangId == julia) && flatppl.embeddedActive)
editorLangId == flatppl || ((editorLangId == python || editorLangId == julia) && flatppl.embeddedActive)
editorLangId == flatppl || ((editorLangId == python || editorLangId == julia) && flatppl.embeddedActive)

flatppl.visualization.sampleCount.markdownDescription: Number of Monte Carlo samples drawn per binding when the visualizer renders a histogram. Higher values give smoother plots; lower values are faster. Changing this clears the visualizer's sample cache.

flatppl.visualization.marginalizationSam.markdownDescription: Monte Carlo sample count `M` used to **marginalize internal latent draws** when estimating an *intractable* density — a likelihood or posterior whose model marginalizes a latent (e.g. `densityof(L, pars)` for a generative model where the observation marginalizes a hidden draw). Each density value averages the tractable conditional over `M` draws of the marginalized latent (the §6 `logsumexp − log M` estimator). This is **separate from `sampleCount`**: the inner marginalization integral usually needs far fewer samples than the outer histogram (≈100 is typically enough; raise it if the likelihood looks noisy in the tails). Changing this clears the visualizer's sample cache.

flatppl.visualization.dagNavigationHisto.markdownDescription: Maximum number of entries kept in the visualizer's DAG navigation history (the back-button stack). Older entries are dropped when the cap is reached. Each entry is a small sub-DAG snapshot (~few KB).

flatppl.visualization.truncateRejectionB.markdownDescription: Per-atom rejection-redraw budget for `truncate(M, S)` when the parent measure isn't CDF-invertible. The visualizer redraws from the underlying distribution up to this many times per atom before giving up and emitting `NaN`. Higher values trade compute for less ESS loss on tightly-truncated measures; lower values keep large-N plots responsive. Changing this clears the visualizer's sample cache.

flatppl.server.path.markdownDescription: Absolute path to a `flatppl-lsp` binary to use instead of the one bundled with the extension. Leave empty to use the bundled per-platform binary. Useful for running a locally built server (`cargo build --release -p flatppl-lsp`).

flatppl.catalogues.path.markdownDescription: Absolute path to a directory of external standard-module catalogue files (`*.ron`). Every `*.ron` in this directory is read and supplied to the language server, so third-party modules' distribution signatures resolve in diagnostics, hover, and completion. Leave empty to use only the built-in catalogue. Changes restart the language server.

flatppl.inference.show.markdownDescription:
Show each binding's inferred type as a CodeLens (`| type`) on the line above it. Off by default. Toggle with the editor-title button or the `FlatPPL: Toggle Inference Display` command.

**Note:** this flag controls *display only*. When off, the language server still computes the inferred types (it just isn't shown). Disabling the inference computation itself is a separate, future server-side change.
